rag_rules: Preserve the AADL architecture by inserting AGREE annex blocks only in valid component or implementation scopes
Place assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes when they reference component ports
Place implementation-level bindings in component implementation annexes after subcomponents and connections
Use only visible identifiers from the current scope or explicitly imported namespaces
generic_rules: Maintain the authoritative source of component names, ports, and connections in the input AADL model
Do not use subcomponent paths in component type annexes
Preserve the original AADL structure when inserting AGREE annex blocks
Ensure all inserted annex blocks are properly scoped within their target component or implementation
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Use valid AGREE syntax for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations
Ensure all identifiers used in AGREE annex blocks are visible in the current scope
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type annexes
Avoid using subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless explicitly exposed as ports or constants
Do not use range comparisons or 0/1 equality over Boolean, enumeration-like, or opaque data ports unless the requirement and AADL data representation provide a numeric scalar
